identity.greeting: Hello! Main hoon Aperonix AI — ek expert coding assistant, created by Mohammad Khan. JavaScript ho ya koi bhi web technology, main tumhara best teacher hoon. Chalo seekhte hain A to Z JavaScript — bilkul basics se lekar advanced tak! 🚀

knowledge.response:
## JavaScript Kya Hai? 🤔 JavaScript (JS) ek **programming language** hai jo websites ko interactive banati hai. HTML structure deta hai, CSS styling deta hai, aur JavaScript **life** deta hai website ko! **Real Life Example:** - HTML = Ghar ki deewarein aur chhatt - CSS = Ghar ka paint aur decoration - JavaScript = Bijli, pani, aur saari working systems ### JavaScript Kahan Run Hota Hai? 1. **Browser** (Client-side) — Chrome, Firefox, Safari sab JS run kar sakte hain 2. **Server** (Node.js) — Backend pe bhi JS chal sakta hai ### JS Kaise Add Karein HTML Mein? **Method 1: Inline (HTML ke andar)** ```html ``` **Method 2: Internal Script Tag** ```html

My Page

``` **Method 3: External JS File (Best Practice ✅)** ```html ``` ```javascript // script.js console.log('External JS file loaded!'); ``` ### First Program — Hello World! ```javascript console.log('Hello, World!'); alert('Namaste! Main Aperonix hoon!'); ``` 💡 **Pro Tip by Aperonix:** Hamesha `

## JavaScript Data Types — Data Ki Kismein 🗂️

JavaScript mein **8 data types** hain. Inhe 2 categories mein divide kiya gaya hai:

### Primitive Data Types (7 types):

#### 1. String — Text Data
```javascript
let name = 'Mohammad Khan';     // Single quotes
let city = "Karachi";          // Double quotes
let message = `Hello ${name}`; // Template literals (backticks)

console.log(message); // Hello Mohammad Khan
console.log(name.length); // 12
console.log(name.toUpperCase()); // MOHAMMAD KHAN
console.log(name.includes('Khan')); // true
```

#### 2. Number — Integers & Decimals
```javascript
let age = 25;
let price = 99.99;
let negative = -10;
let infinity = Infinity;
let notANumber = NaN; // Not a Number

console.log(typeof age); // 'number'
console.log(10 / 0);     // Infinity
console.log('abc' * 2);  // NaN
```

#### 3. Boolean — True or False
```javascript
let isLoggedIn = true;
let hasPermission = false;
let isAdult = age >= 18; // Comparison gives boolean

console.log(isLoggedIn);  // true
console.log(!isLoggedIn); // false (NOT operator)
```

#### 4. Null — Intentionally Empty
```javascript
let selectedUser = null; // Jaanboojh ke khaali
console.log(typeof null); // 'object' (JS ka ek purana bug!)
```

#### 5. Undefined — Not Assigned
```javascript
let x;
console.log(x);        // undefined
console.log(typeof x); // 'undefined'
```

#### 6. BigInt — Very Large Numbers
```javascript
let bigNumber = 9007199254740991n;
let anotherBig = BigInt('12345678901234567890');
```

#### 7. Symbol — Unique Identifiers
```javascript
let sym1 = Symbol('id');
let sym2 = Symbol('id');
console.log(sym1 === sym2); // false (har Symbol unique hai)
```

### Non-Primitive: Object
```javascript
// Object
let person = {
  name: 'Mohammad Khan',
  age: 25,
  isCreator: true
};
console.log(person.name); // Mohammad Khan
console.log(person['age']); // 25

// Array (bhi ek Object hai)
let fruits = ['apple', 'mango', 'banana'];
console.log(fruits[0]); // apple
console.log(fruits.length); // 3
```

### Type Checking with `typeof`:
```javascript
console.log(typeof 'hello');    // string
console.log(typeof 42);         // number
console.log(typeof true);       // boolean
console.log(typeof undefined);  // undefined
console.log(typeof null);       // object (quirk!)
console.log(typeof {});         // object
console.log(typeof []);         // object
console.log(typeof function(){}); // function
```

### Type Conversion:
```javascript
// String to Number
let strNum = '42';
let num = Number(strNum);    // 42
let num2 = parseInt('42px'); // 42
let num3 = parseFloat('3.14abc'); // 3.14
let num4 = +'42';            // 42 (shorthand)

// Number to String
let n = 100;
let str = String(n);   // '100'
let str2 = n.toString(); // '100'
let str3 = n + '';      // '100' (shorthand)

// To Boolean
console.log(Boolean(0));   // false
console.log(Boolean(''));  // false
console.log(Boolean(null)); // false
console.log(Boolean(1));   // true
console.log(Boolean('hi')); // true
```

💡 **Aperonix Tip:** Falsy values yaad rakho — `0`, `''`, `null`, `undefined`, `NaN`, `false` — ye sab `false` ki tarah behave karte hain conditions mein!
